message:
fix(core): harden data structures against corrupt/deserialized state and arithmetic edge cases

Replace debug_assert panics and bare unwrap calls with graceful runtime
handling for states that can arise from deserialization, stale data, or
unusual inputs:

- adaptive_radix_tree: Node48 insert_child promotes to Node256 when all
  slots occupied (stale count field) instead of unwrapping None
- bloom_filter: guard all operations against num_bits==0 / num_hashes==0
  to prevent division-by-zero from deserialized empty filters
- persistent_ds: VersionedStore::push clamps regressed timestamps to
  preserve binary-search sort invariant instead of debug_assert panic
- runtime: percentile_from_samples bounds index for percentile 0 or >100
- rate_limit_tracker: use checked_mul to prevent u64 overflow in
  parse_retry_after with enormous values
- session_restore: replace unwrap with let-else for defensive candidate
  extraction
- watchdog: clamp zero history_capacity to 1 and respect configured
  capacity instead of hardcoded 1000
- storage: FtVersion::parse rejects versions with >3 numeric components

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
